docs.title: Useful command to get the nbt of the blockentity/entity.
docs.text:
It's inevitable that when you're writing a scene you'll come across some blocks/entities that require nbt to be set.

while you can get it by /data get block/entity you can't copy it in the game.

Ldlib provides a command to help you get the nbt of a block/entity in your game and copy it to the clipboard.

/ldlib copy_block_tag [pos] / /ldlib copy_entity_tag [entity_selector]: chat panel will show the tag when you execute this command. and you mouse click the [Copy to clipboard] in the chat panel to copy to the clipboard.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
LDLib provides a wide range of ready-to-use components, allowing you to create GUI with advanced UI controls. Additionally, LDLib offers a visual editor, enabling you to design UIs without writing a single line of code.

We provide two methods for creating UI:

  1. Java / KubeJS
  2. UI Editor

Of course, you can also combine both methods. For example, you can use the UI Editor to design the UI and then use Java or KubeJS to add interaction logic—which is our recommended approach.

Refer to the Widgets to check all widgets in details.

docs.title: Get Started
docs.text:
Creating UI using Java and KubeJS is mostly the same. This page will introduce the basic workflow of creating and using a UI.

The entire UI creation and usage pipeline consists of the following steps:

  1. Create UI widget and layout (1)

    1. Create buttons and item slots, set their positions...

  2. Bind UI functional logic (1)

    1. Add logic to be executed when a button is clicked, bind inventory to item slots...

  3. Display the UI (1)

    1. Open the GUI when right-clicking an item, open the GUI when right-clicking a block...

Technically, the UI function binding and UI widget creation processes happen simultaneously, as many controls provide constructors that bind functions at the same time.

On this page, we separate step 1 and step 2 in the code examples to provide a clearer understanding of how the UI works.

docs.title: Create UI Widgets and Layout
docs.text:
Let's begin with a WigetGroup, which is a container of child widgtes. Therefore, we create a WidgetGroup as a root widget. Check all widgets here.

Then, we add a Label and a Button into it.

JavaKubeJS 
public WidgetGroup createUI() {
    // create a root container
    var root = new WidgetGroup();
    root.setSize(100, 100);
    root.setBackground(ResourceBorderTexture.BORDERED_BACKGROUND);

    // create a label and a button
    var label = new LabelWidget();
    label.setSelfPosition(20, 20);
    label.setText("Hello, World!");
    var button = new ButtonWidget();
    button.setSelfPosition(20, 60);
    button.setSize(60, 20);
    // prepare button textures
    var backgroundImage = ResourceBorderTexture.BUTTON_COMMON;
    var hoverImage = backgroundImage.copy().setColor(ColorPattern.CYAN.color);
    var textAbove = new TextTexture("Click me!");
    button.setButtonTexture(backgroundImage, textAbove);
    button.setClickedTexture(hoverImage, textAbove);

    // add the label and button to the root container
    root.addWidgets(label, button);
    return root;
}

function createUI() {
    // create a root container
    let root = new WidgetGroup();
    root.setSize(100, 100);
    root.setBackground(ResourceBorderTexture.BORDERED_BACKGROUND);

    // create a label and a button
    let label = new LabelWidget();
    label.setSelfPosition(20, 20);
    label.setText("Hello, World!");
    let button = new ButtonWidget();
    button.setSelfPosition(20, 60);
    button.setSize(60, 20);
    // prepare button textures
    let backgroundImage = ResourceBorderTexture.BUTTON_COMMON;
    let hoverImage = backgroundImage.copy().setColor(ColorPattern.CYAN.color);
    let textAbove = new TextTexture("Click me!");
    button.setButtonTexture(backgroundImage, textAbove);
    button.setClickedTexture(hoverImage, textAbove);

    // add the label and button to the root container
    root.addWidgets(label, button);
    return root;
}
Your browser does not support video.

docs.title: Bind UI functional logic
docs.text:
After the creation of the ui, we should implement the logic of the ui. For example, we want to click the button to change the label text.

JavaKubeJS 
public WidgetGroup createUI() {
    // creation of the ui
    // ....

    // click logic
    AtomicInteger counter = new AtomicInteger(0);
    button.setOnPressCallback(clickData -> {
        label.setText("Clicked " + counter.incrementAndGet() + " times!");
    });

    return root;
}

function createUI() {
    // creation of the ui
    // ....

    // click logic
    let counter = 0;
    button.setOnPressCallback(clickData => {
        counter++;
        label.setText("Clicked " + counter + " times!");
    });

    return root;
}
Your browser does not support video.

docs.title: Display the UI
docs.text:
Now, let's display the ui we create! We need to specify a UI Factory to display the ui, which maintains the lifecycle of the ui.

There are four steps:

  1. open ui
  2. create UI in server side
  3. notify to open UI
  4. create UI in remote side
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Note left of Server: 1. open UI
  Note left of Server: 2. create UI in server side
  Server->>Remote: 3. notify to open UI
  Note right of Remote: 4. create UI in remote side

UI Factory will help handle the step 3. Therefore, user should define WHEN to trigger step 1, and WHAT to be createed in step 2 and step 4. In general, the UI created on the server side and the remote side is the same in most cases.

LDLib provides two built-in factories:

  1. Block Entity UI Factory
  2. Held Item UI Factory

docs.title: Block Entity UI Factory
docs.text: This factory allows user to open the ui from a block.

docs.title: Java
docs.text:
1. Java user should implement IUIHolder.Block for your own BlockEntity and implement the method createUI(Player entityPlayer).

  2. Call the method BlockEntityUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI() when you want to open the ui.

docs.title: KubeJS
docs.text:
KubeJS user can do the same thing in an easy way. User can even open the ui for the block (without entity), but less accessibility compared with Java.

  1. KubeJS user should use LDLibUI.block(ui_name, e => {}) to create the ui by the given ui_name.
  2. Call the method BlockUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI(player, pos, ui_name) when you want to open the ui.
JavaKubeJS 
public class TestBlockEntity extends BlockEntity implements IUIHolder {

    public void onPlayerUse(Player player) {
        // step 1 here.
        if (player instanceof ServerPlayer serverPlayer) {
            BlockEntityUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI(this, serverPlayer);
        }
    }

    private WidgetGroup createUI() {
        // ....
    }

    @Override
    public ModularUI createUI(Player entityPlayer) {
        // step 2 and step 4 here 
        return new ModularUI(createUI(), this, entityPlayer);
    }
}

// server script

BlockEvents.rightClicked('test_block_ui', event => { 
    // step 1 here.
    BlockUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI(event.player, event.block.pos, "test_block_ui");
})

function createUI() {
    // ....
}

LDLibUI.block("test_block_ui", e => { 
    // step 2 and step 4 here 

    // let level = e.level
    // let pos = e.pos
    // let block = e.block
    // let player = e.player

    var ui = createUI();
    e.success(ui);
})

docs.title: Held Item UI Factory
docs.text: This factory allows user to open the ui from the held item.

docs.title: Java
docs.text:
1. Java user should implement IUIHolder.Item for your own Item and implement the method createUI(Player entityPlayer, HeldItemUIFactory.HeldItemHolder holder).

  2. Call the method HeldItemUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI() when you want to open the ui.

docs.title: KubeJS
docs.text:
KubeJS user can do the same thing in an easy way. User can even open the ui for the block (without entity), but less accessibility compared with Java.

  1. KubeJS user should use LDLibUI.item(ui_name, e => {}) to create the ui by the given ui_name.
  2. Call the method ItemUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI(player, hand, ui_name) when you want to open the ui.
JavaKubeJS 
public class TestItem implements IUIHolder.Item {
    @Override
    public InteractionResult useOn(UseOnContext context) {
        // step 1 here.
        if (context.getPlayer() instanceof ServerPlayer serverPlayer) {
            HeldItemUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI(serverPlayer, context.getHand());
        }
        return InteractionResult.SUCCESS;
    }

    private WidgetGroup createUI() {
        // ....
    }

    @Override
    public ModularUI createUI(Player entityPlayer, HeldItemUIFactory.HeldItemHolder holder) {
        // step 2 and step 4
        return new ModularUI(createUI(), holder, entityPlayer);
    }
}

// server script

ItemEvents.firstRightClicked('minecraft:stick', event => {
    // step 1 here.
    ItemUIFactory.INSTANCE.openUI(event.player, event.hand, "test_item_ui");
})

function createUI() {
    // ....
}

LDLibUI.item("test_item_ui", e => {
    // step 2 and step 4

    // let player = e.player
    // let hand = e.hand
    // let held = e.held

    var ui = createUI();
    e.success(ui);
})
Your browser does not support video.

docs.title: Load UI from file
docs.text:
Through creating UI by code has more flexibility, using UI Editor instead is more intuitive and easy for novice. The ideal approach is to design the ui layout via the Editor and bind UI functional logic via code.

before reading

Make sure you has basic knowledge of how to create the ui via UI Editor and prepare a ui file for loading.

Loading ui file is really easy, just few lines code. We suppose you have a ui file with the location .minecraft/ldlib/assets/ldlib/projects/ui/test_ui.ui.

You can get a creator via the method UIProject.loadUIFromFile(location). creator caches the resources to speed up the creation process which should be stored for the same project loading.

Warning

creator may be null if the ui file could not be loaded.

JavaKubeJS 
public WidgetGroup createUI() {
    var creator = UIProject.loadUIFromFile(new ResourceLocation("ldlib:test_ui"));
    // creator caches the resources to speed up the creation process.
    // you should better store it for the same project loading.
    return creator.get();
}

function createUI() {
    let creator = UIProject.loadUIFromFile("ldlib:test_ui");
    // creator caches the resources to speed up the creation process.
    // you should better store it for the same project loading.
    return creator.get();
}

docs.title: Bind UI functional logic
docs.text:
After loading the ui project, we should bind the functional logic for it. First, we should get the instance of the widget. LDLib provide two methods to obtain it:

  1. List<Widget> getWidgetsById(regex): Get all widgets based on a regex pattern and return all of them.
  2. Widget getFirstWidgetById(regex): Get all widgets based on a regex pattern and return the first one.

Note

Do not forget to assign an id to the widgets you need.

Warning

Do not include the file extension in the resourceLocation of the UI File! i.e. .minecraft/ldlib/assets/ldlib/projects/ui/test_ui.ui translates to ldlib:test_ui

JavaKubeJS 
public WidgetGroup createUI() {
    var creator = UIProject.loadUIFromFile(new ResourceLocation("ldlib:test_ui"));
    // creator caches the resources to speed up the creation process.
    // you should better store it for the same project loading.
    var root = creator.get();

    var button = root.getFirstWidgetById("button_id");
    if (button != null) {
        button.setOnPressCallback(clickData -> {
            System.out.println(clickData.isShiftClick);
        });
    }

    return root;
}

function createUI() {
    let creator = UIProject.loadUIFromFile("ldlib:test_ui");
    // creator caches the resources to speed up the creation process.
    // you should better store it for the same project loading.
    let root = creator.get();

    let button = root.getFirstWidgetById("button_id");
    if (button != null) {
        button.setOnPressCallback(clickData => {
            console.log(clickData.isShiftClick);
        });
    }

    return root;
}

docs.title: Preliminary
docs.text: GUI Texture is widely used for background setup, image dispaly, etc. LDLib provides lots of different textures. Textures have some generic functions.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description xOffset Horizontal offset yOffset Vertical offset scale Scale factor (default is 1) rotation Rotation angle in degrees

docs.title: rotate
docs.text:
Sets the rotation angle.

Java / KubeJS 
texture.rotate(45);

docs.title: scale
docs.text:
Sets the scale factor.

Java / KubeJS 
texture.scale(1.5);

docs.title: transform
docs.text:
Sets the horizontal and vertical offset.

Java / KubeJS 
texture.transform(10, 20);

docs.title: copy
docs.text:
Creates a copy of the texture.

Java / KubeJS 
var copiedTexture = texture.copy();

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description imageLocation The resource location for the image cellSize The size of each cell in the texture grid from The starting cell index for animation to The ending cell index for animation animation The animation speed value color The color overlay applied to the texture

docs.title: setTexture
docs.text:
Sets the texture

Java / KubeJS 
animationTexture.setTexture("ldlib:textures/gui/particles.png");

docs.title: setCellSize
docs.text:
Sets the cell size. Refer to how many cells does the animation texture need to be divided into (side length).

Java / KubeJS 
animationTexture.setCellSize(8);

docs.title: setAnimation
docs.text:
Sets the animation range from which cell to which cell.

Java / KubeJS 
animationTexture.setAnimation(32, 44);

docs.title: setAnimation
docs.text:
Sets the animation speed. Tick time between cells.

Java / KubeJS 
animationTexture.setAnimation(1);

docs.title: setColor
docs.text:
Sets the texture color.

Java / KubeJS 
animationTexture.setColor(0xff000000);

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description color The color applied to the border border The border width radiusLTInner Top-left inner corner radius radiusLBInner Bottom-left inner corner radius radiusRTInner Top-right inner corner radius radiusRBInner Bottom-right inner corner radius radiusLTOuter Top-left outer corner radius radiusLBOuter Bottom-left outer corner radius radiusRTOuter Top-right outer corner radius radiusRBOuter Bottom-right outer corner radius

docs.title: setBorder
docs.text:
Sets the border width.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setBorder(5);

docs.title: setColor
docs.text:
Sets the border color.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setColor(0xff00ff);

docs.title: setRadius
docs.text:
Sets uniform inner and outer radii based on the provided radius and current border.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setRadius(10);

docs.title: setLeftRadius
docs.text:
Sets the left-side radii for both inner and outer corners.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setLeftRadius(8);

docs.title: setRightRadius
docs.text:
Sets the right-side radii for both inner and outer corners.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setRightRadius(8);

docs.title: setTopRadius
docs.text:
Sets the top-side radii for both inner and outer corners.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setTopRadius(8);

docs.title: setBottomRadius
docs.text:
Sets the bottom-side radii for both inner and outer corners.

Java / KubeJS 
colorBorderTexture.setBottomRadius(8);

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description textures An array of textures used in the group

docs.title: setTextures
docs.text:
Sets the textures used in the group.

Java / KubeJS 
guiTextureGroup.setTextures(texture1, texture2);

docs.title: setColor
docs.text:
Sets the color for all textures in the group.

Java / KubeJS 
guiTextureGroup.setColor(0xff00ff);

docs.title: ItemStackTexture
docs.text: ItemStackTexture will display items in a loop.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description items Array of item stacks used by the texture color Color overlay applied to the item stack texture

docs.title: setItems
docs.text:
Sets the items.

Java / KubeJS 
itemStackTexture.setItems(item1, item2);

docs.title: setColor
docs.text:
Sets the color overlay for the item stack texture.

Java / KubeJS 
itemStackTexture.setColor(0xffff00ff);

docs.title: ProgressTexture
docs.text: The ProgressTexture is used to render a progress bar texture that fills according to a specified direction. It combines an empty and a filled texture area to visually represent progress.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description fillDirection The direction in which the progress fills emptyBarArea Texture used for the empty portion of the progress bar filledBarArea Texture used for the filled portion of the progress bar progress The current progress value (0.0 to 1.0)

docs.title: setTexture
docs.text:
Sets the progress textures. emptyBarArea and filledBarArea can any type of the GUi Texture.

Java / KubeJS 
progressTexture.setTexture(emptyBarArea, filledBarArea);

docs.title: setProgress
docs.text:
Sets the progress value.

Java / KubeJS 
progressTexture.setProgress(0.75);

docs.title: setFillDirection
docs.text:
Sets the fill direction for the progress bar.

Java / KubeJS 
progressTexture.setFillDirection(ProgressTexture.FillDirection.RIGHT_TO_LEFT);

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description color The color applied to the rectangle radiusLT Top-left corner radius radiusLB Bottom-left corner radius radiusRT Top-right corner radius radiusRB Bottom-right corner radius

docs.title: setRadius
docs.text:
Sets a uniform radius for all corners.

Java / KubeJS 
colorRectTexture.setRadius(10);

docs.title: setLeftRadius
docs.text:
Sets the left-side radii (top and bottom) for the rectangle.

Java / KubeJS 
colorRectTexture.setLeftRadius(8);

docs.title: setRightRadius
docs.text:
Sets the right-side radii (top and bottom) for the rectangle.

Java / KubeJS 
colorRectTexture.setRightRadius(8);

docs.title: setTopRadius
docs.text:
Sets the top-side radii (left and right) for the rectangle.

Java / KubeJS 
colorRectTexture.setTopRadius(8);

docs.title: setBottomRadius
docs.text:
Sets the bottom-side radii (left and right) for the rectangle.

Java / KubeJS 
colorRectTexture.setBottomRadius(8);

docs.title: ResourceTexture
docs.text: The ResourceTexture class extends TransformTexture to render textures based on a resource location. It supports configurable offsets, dimensions, and color overlays.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description imageLocation The resource location of the texture image offsetX Horizontal offset of the texture (default is 0) offsetY Vertical offset of the texture (default is 0) imageWidth Width factor of the texture (default is 1) imageHeight Height factor of the texture (default is 1) color Color overlay applied to the texture (default is -1)

docs.title: createTexture
docs.text:
Create a texture from a resource location.

Java / KubeJS 
// Using float parameters
var texture = new ResourceTexture("ldlib:textures/gui/icon.png");

docs.title: getSubTexture
docs.text:
Returns a sub-texture of the current texture.

Java / KubeJS 
// Using float parameters
var subTexture = resourceTexture.getSubTexture(0.2, 0.2, 0.5, 0.5);

docs.title: ResourceBorderTexture
docs.text: The ResourceBorderTexture class extends ResourceTexture to render textures with configurable borders. It calculates relative sizes for corners, edges, and the central area, enabling detailed customization for UI backgrounds and buttons.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description boderSize The size of the border corners imageSize The overall size of the texture image imageLocation The resource location of the texture image

docs.title: setBoderSize
docs.text:
Sets the size of the border corners.

Java / KubeJS 
resourceBorderTexture.setBoderSize(5, 5);

docs.title: setImageSize
docs.text:
Sets the overall size of the texture image.

Java / KubeJS 
resourceBorderTexture.setImageSize(200, 150);

docs.title: ShaderTexture
docs.text:
The ShaderTexture class extends TransformTexture to render textures using custom shaders. It supports dynamic shader updates, setting uniform values, and binding textures for advanced visual effects. The class provides methods for creating shader-based textures from resource locations or raw shader code, along with caching and disposal mechanisms.

IMPORTANT!!!

If you are using raw shader, DO NOT forget to release the texture after using.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description location The resource location of the shader resolution The shader resolution factor (affects the iResolution uniform) color Color overlay applied to the shader texture

docs.title: dispose
docs.text:
Dispose shader.

Java / KubeJS 
shaderTexture.dispose();

docs.title: updateShader
docs.text:
Updates the shader using a new resource location.

Java / KubeJS 
shaderTexture.updateShader(new ResourceLocation("modid:shader"));

docs.title: updateRawShader
docs.text:
Updates the shader using raw shader code.

Java / KubeJS 
shaderTexture.updateRawShader("raw shader code");

docs.title: setUniformCache
docs.text:
Sets a uniform cache consumer to update additional shader uniforms.

Java / KubeJS 
shaderTexture.setUniformCache(cache -> {
    // Update additional uniforms as needed
});

docs.title: setResolution
docs.text:
Sets the resolution factor used in the shader.

Java / KubeJS 
shaderTexture.setResolution(2.5f);

docs.title: TextTexture
docs.text: The TextTexture class extends TransformTexture to render text as a texture. It supports dynamic text updates through a supplier, configurable text styling (color, background color, drop shadow), and various text alignment or animation types (e.g. roll, hide). This class is primarily used to display formatted and localized text within GUI components.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description text The formatted text to display color The text color (modifiable via setter) backgroundColor The background color behind the text width The maximum width for wrapping the text rollSpeed The speed at which text rolls (for animated text types) dropShadow Whether a drop shadow is applied to the text type The text display type (e.g., NORMAL, ROLL, HIDE, LEFT, RIGHT) supplier A supplier for dynamic text updates

docs.title: setSupplier
docs.text:
Sets a supplier to provide dynamic text updates.

JavaKubeJS 
textTexture.setSupplier(() -> "Updated dynamic text");

textTexture.setSupplier(() => "Updated dynamic text");

docs.title: updateText
docs.text:
Updates the displayed text. This method is invoked automatically via the supplier or can be called directly.

Java / KubeJS 
textTexture.updateText("New Text Content");

docs.title: setBackgroundColor
docs.text:
Sets the background color behind the text.

Java / KubeJS 
textTexture.setBackgroundColor(0xffff0000);

docs.title: setDropShadow
docs.text:
Enables or disables the drop shadow effect on the text.

Java / KubeJS 
textTexture.setDropShadow(true);

docs.title: setWidth
docs.text:
Sets the maximum width for the text area. This method also recalculates text wrapping based on the new width.

Java / KubeJS 
textTexture.setWidth(100);

docs.title: setType
docs.text:
Sets the text display type (e.g., NORMAL, ROLL, LEFT_HIDE).

TextType

  • NORMAL: center, add new lines below
  • HIDE: center, hide redundant words
  • ROLL: center, hide redundant words, roll words while hover
  • ROLL_ALWAYS: center always roll words while redundant
  • LEFT: same as NOMAL but left align
  • RIGHT: same as NORMAL but right aligh
  • LEFT_HIDE: same as HIDE but left align
  • LEFT_ROLL: same as ROLL but left align
  • LEFT_ROLL_ALWAYS: same as ROLL_ALWAYS but let align
Java / KubeJS 
textTexture.setType(TextType.ROLL);

docs.title: Get Start
docs.text: The UI Editor is a powerful built-in visual editor provided by ldlib. You can use it to easily design your UI, use it in your own mods, or use it through Java / KubeJS.

docs.title: How to open the UI Editor
docs.text: In general, you can open it by command/ldlib ui_editor, which will use ./minecraft/ldlib/.. as its workspace.

docs.title: Main Screen
docs.text:
1. Menu: New/Save/Open UI projects. You can also import/export Resources here.
  2. Configurator: Basically all the setup happens here.
  3. Resources: available resources, e.g. color, texture, lang entries.

docs.title: Create a new project
docs.text:
When you first use it, click it to create an empty UI project. (If you are making UI for MBD, select the MBD project)

No surprise, you'll see the follow case.

  1. ToolBox: Contains all the available UI widgets(components).
  2. Root Widget: The whole project has one and only one root widget, which is created by the system and you cannot delete it.

docs.title: 2. Basic
docs.text:
1. red frame are selected widgets.
  2. blue frame is the widget that mouse is hovering over it.

docs.title: Multi-Select
docs.text: Press ctrl to multi-select / cancle-select widget.

docs.title: Drag Selected Widgets
docs.text:
1. Hold down alt + left-clicked, if you see the arrows (all directions) then you can drag it.
  2. Anyway, you could also modify position by a configurator.

docs.title: Scale Selected Widgets
docs.text:
1. Hold down alt + right-clicked, if you see the arrows (right bottom) then you can scale it.
  2. Anyway, you could also modify size by a configurator.

docs.title: Add a Widget
docs.text:
All widgets (except the Root) need to be added to a father (parent) widget that accepts it, and we call such father (parent) widget as Group Type widget (e.g. Group, Tab Group, Scrollable Group).

  1. You can find all available widgets in the toolbox.
  2. Drag a widget into a Group Type widget.
  3. Rendering a green frame if such widget can accept it.

docs.title: Move widget from one Groug to another Group
docs.text:
Sometimes you may want to modify the parent control.

  1. You can do this by the menu (right-clicking page) and cut/copy to the selected parent widget.
  2. A better way is to press the shiftand move it into the new Group.

Rendering a green frame if such widget can accept it.

docs.title: Adjust Children Widgets Order
docs.text: In general, all the Group widgets have a children tab in its configurator, showing all children, you can adjust their order by dragging.

docs.title: Menu
docs.text:
Right-click the page to open the menu.

  1. All operations will be performed on selected widgets.
  2. Basic operations
  3. Align: availabe when you select multi widgets.

docs.title: Group Widget
docs.text:
Group refers to widgets to which can accept child widgets

There are three built-in widgets so far: Group, Scrollable Group, and Tab Group.

docs.title: Group
docs.text:
Group is the most common widget, Transform will be applied to its children, and deletion and copy of the group are also applied to its children.

You can drag child's preview to adjust their order

docs.title: Scrollable Group
docs.text:
Children in a Scrollable Group are hidden when they exceed the its size and allow users to scroll or drag groups to adjust the view.

The configurator has a lot of settings such as scroll bar styles, whether you can drag, etc. You can try them out yourself.

docs.title: Tab Group
docs.text: As the name implies, Tab group allows you to set multiple tabs, which you can switch between different group views.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
The UI Project contains three built-in resources: textures, colors and lang entries. Resources are owned by projects and cannot be shared between projects.

If you want the current project's resources to be available in other projects, you can export the resources first and then open other projects to import it.

docs.title: Textrures
docs.text:
1. Stored textures.
  2. Add new textures, glad we support many types of textures so far :).
  3. It's worth noting that empty is a built-in texture and you can't delete it.

docs.title: Use it
docs.text:
When you open the configuration of a widget, you find that some where can accept the texture (e.g. background of Basic Info), and you can drag the texture in.

  1. Drag texture.
  2. Rendering a green frame if it can accept this texture.

NOTE: When you drag it to a widget, it replaces its background image by default.

docs.title: Edit Texture
docs.text:
Right-click to open the menu and edit the selected texture.

1. Open its configurator. 2. Modify the texture type. In general you'd better not modify it, because switching type will not modify references of previous one. 3. Some textures provide a preview of the settings, and you can open the file selector by clicking on it. 4. You can scale, translate, and rotate the texture by setting its Transform.

NOTE: You can use GroupTexture with transform to create a more complex texture.

1. Combine multi textures. 2. Add a new layer.

NOTE: You can create an animation texture by setting the starting and ending of frames, and the interval time.

docs.title: Use it
docs.text:
When you open a configuration, you find that some where can accept the number (e.g. color of the label widget), and you can drag the color in.

  1. Drag color.
  2. Rendering a green frame if it can accept this color.

NOTE: When you drag it to a widget, it replaces its background to a Color Texture by default.

docs.title: Edit Color
docs.text:
Right-click to open the menu and edit the selected color.

  1. Pickup color (HSB mode), you can right click the pallete to swith mode.
  2. Preview.
  3. Modify by argb.

docs.title: Entries
docs.text:
Enties stores key-value, which can be regard as a lang file.

When you open a configuration, you find that some where can accept the number (e.g. color of the label widget), and you can drag the color in. You can fill in your localization data, and then export to the lang file directly (W.I.P)

docs.title: Use it
docs.text:
1. Drag entries.
  2. Rendering a green frame if it can accept a string.

NOTE: When you drag it to a widget, it replaces its hover tooltips by default.

docs.title: Edit Entries
docs.text:
Right-click to open the menu and edit its vaule.

  1. Typing your text here.

You can right-click to open the menu and rename its key.

docs.title: Preliminary
docs.text:
LDLib provides a wide range of widgets. You can review their functionalities and APIs on their respective pages. In this section, we introduce the fundamental concepts that underlie all widgets, ensuring you have a solid understanding of their common principles.

All widgets inherit from the Widget class. As a result, they all share a set of universal APIs.

docs.title: Basic properties
docs.text:
All properties can be accessed via Java / KubeJS.

JavaKubeJS 
var id = widget.getId();
var pos = widget.getPosition();
widget.setSize(10, 32);
widget.setSlefPosition(10, 10);

let id = widget.getId();
let pos = widget.position // getPosition() is also fine.
widget.setSize(10, 32);
widget.setSlefPosition(10, 10);
Field Description id Widget id, it doesn't have to be unique，and can be empty. selftPosition Self position represents the relative local position in the parent widget parentPosition Parent widget's global position. position Global position in the window, which is computed by the self position and parent position. size Widget size, this property affect the rect collid dection, e.g. hover, click, etc. isVisible Whether the widget is visible, only affect rendering, logic is still working. isActive Whether the widget logic is working. align align position refer to the parent. backgroundTexture background texture. hoverTexture darw texture while mouse hover. overlay overlay texture above the background texture. parent parent widget. align align position refer to the parent.

about position

Position is an important concept. Check the image below:

docs.title: setHoverTooltips()
docs.text:
Used to define the tooltips while the mouse hovered. It support both string and component as input.

Your browser does not support video. Java / KubeJS 
widget.setHoverTooltips("this is a button");
// widget.setHoverTooltips("line 1", "line2");

Info

LDLib provides massive different types of textures, choose what you want:). See GUI Textures to check supported textures.

docs.title: setBackground()
docs.text: Used to set the background texture of the widget.

docs.title: setHoverTexture()
docs.text:
Used to set the hover texture of the widget.

Java / KubeJS 
widget.setBackground(new ResourceTexture("ldlib:textures/gui/icon.png"));

docs.title: isMouseOverElement()
docs.text:
TO check whether the mouse over the widget.

Java / KubeJS 
widget.isMouseOverElement(mouseX, mouseY);

docs.title: ButtonWidget
docs.text: The ButtonWidget is a UI widget representing a clickable button in the GUI system

docs.title: Basic properties
docs.text:
All properties can be accessed via Java / KubeJS.

Field Description isClicked is the button clicked currently

docs.title: setButtonTexture()
docs.text:
Equal to the setBackground.

Java / KubeJS 
button.setButtonTexture(ResourceBorderTexture.BUTTON_COMMON, new TextTexture("Button"));

docs.title: setClickedTexture()
docs.text:
Remove child widget from it.

Java / KubeJS 
button.setClickedTexture(ResourceBorderTexture.BUTTON_COMMON, new TextTexture("Clicked"));

docs.title: setOnPressCallback()
docs.text:
Used to bind functional logic.

ClickData provides mouse state information:

  1. clickData.button: mouse button id number.

    button Description 0 left button 1 right button 2 middle button

  2. clickData.isShiftClick: is the shift key typed.

  3. clickData.isCtrlClick: is the ctrl key typed.
  4. clickData.isRemote: is the remote enverionment.
JavaKubeJS 
button.setOnPressCallback(clickData -> {
    if (clickData.isRemote) { // can be used to check trigger side

    }
});

button.setOnPressCallback(clickData => {
    if (clickData.isRemote) { // can be used to check trigger side

    }
});

docs.title: ImageWidget
docs.text: ImageWidget is used to display images, which provided by a GUI Texture.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description border The border width (range: -100 to 100) borderColor The color of the border

docs.title: setImage
docs.text:
Sets the image texture using a texture instance.

Java / KubeJS 
imageWidget.setImage(new ResourceTexture("ldlib:textures/gui/icon.png"));

docs.title: setImage
docs.text:
Sets the image texture using a supplier.

JavaKubeJS 
imageWidget.setImage(() -> new ResourceTexture("ldlib:textures/gui/icon.png"));

imageWidget.setImage(() => new ResourceTexture("ldlib:textures/gui/icon.png"));

docs.title: getImage
docs.text:
Returns the current image texture.

Java / KubeJS 
var texture = imageWidget.getImage();

docs.title: setBorder
docs.text:
Sets the border width and color.

Java / KubeJS 
imageWidget.setBorder(2, 0xFFFFFFFF); // ARGB

docs.title: LabelWidget
docs.text:
LabelWidget is used to display text and component.

Advanced Widget

LabelWidget is a light-weight widget to display text only. text height, align, ... are fixed. Therefore, we reconmend to use the TextTextureWidget instead, which provids advanced controll of text display.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description color The text color as an integer dropShadow Indicates whether the drop shadow effect is enabled lastTextValue currently text read only

docs.title: setText()
docs.text:
Updates the label text with a string.

Java / KubeJS 
label.setText("New Label Text");

docs.title: setComponent()
docs.text: Same as setText() but support component as input

docs.title: setTextProvider()
docs.text:
Configures a supplier to dynamically provide the label text. It will obtain the latest text per tick.

JavaKubeJS 
label.setTextProvider(() -> "Dynamic Text");

label.setTextProvider(() => "Dynamic Text");

docs.title: setColor()
docs.text:
Sets the text color. If a rich text component is already set, its style will be replaced accordingly.

Java / KubeJS 
label.setColor(0xFFFFFFFF); // ARGB

docs.title: setDropShadow()
docs.text:
Enables or disables the drop shadow effect for the label.

Java / KubeJS 
label.setDropShadow(true);

docs.title: PhantomSlotWidget
docs.text:
Your browser does not support video.

The PhantomSlotWidget is a UI widget representing a "phantom" item slot, commonly used for ghost ingredient inputs in recipe configurations. Unlike regular slots, it does not interact with real inventories but allows setting, modifying, and clearing items for visual or configurational purposes.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Does not allow item taking or putting from real inventories.
  • Supports setting items via UI clicks or API calls.
  • Allows right-click clearing (clearSlotOnRightClick).
  • Supports integration with JEI/EMI for ghost ingredient handling.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description maxStackSize Maximum allowed stack size in this phantom slot. clearSlotOnRightClick Whether right-clicking clears the slot.

docs.title: APIs
docs.text: It owns all APIs from SlotWidget, and you can get or set item by its APIs.

docs.title: setClearSlotOnRightClick
docs.text:
Configures whether right-clicking on the slot clears its contents.

Java / KubeJS 
phantomSlot.setClearSlotOnRightClick(true);

docs.title: setMaxStackSize
docs.text:
Sets the maximum allowed stack size in the phantom slot.

Java / KubeJS 
phantomSlot.setMaxStackSize(64);

docs.title: Mouse Interactions
docs.text:
Phantom slots handle different types of mouse interactions:

Mouse Action Effect Left-click on empty slot with an item Sets the item to the slot Left-click on filled slot with an item Replaces the item in the slot Right-click on filled slot Decreases stack size Shift + Click Adjusts stack size dynamically Right-click on empty slot Clears the slot (if clearSlotOnRightClick is enabled)

docs.title: PhantomTankWidget
docs.text:
Your browser does not support video.

The PhantomTankWidget is a ghost fluid slot that allows setting fluid content without actual transfer mechanics. It's useful for defining recipe inputs or fluid placeholders.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Phantom fluid storage – Doesn't actually consume or provide fluids.
  • Supports drag-and-drop – Accepts fluid items from JEI, EMI, or REI.
  • Custom event handling – Updates an external state when fluid changes.

docs.title: APIs
docs.text: It owns all APIs from TankWidget, and you can get or set item by its APIs.

docs.title: setIFluidStackUpdater
docs.text:
Registers a callback to track fluid changes.

JavaKubeJS 
phantomTank.setIFluidStackUpdater(fluid -> {
    System.out.println("New phantom fluid: " + fluid);
});

phantomTank.setIFluidStackUpdater(fluid => {
    console.log("New phantom fluid: " + fluid);
});

docs.title: SelectorWidget
docs.text:
Your browser does not support video.

The SelectorWidget is a dropdown-style selection widget that allows users to pick an option from a predefined list. It supports dynamically updating the selection list, displaying a configurable UI, and handling selection changes efficiently.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Dropdown selection – Expands to show available choices.
  • Event handling – Fires callbacks when the selection changes.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Field Type Description currentValue String Currently selected option.

docs.title: setCandidates
docs.text:
Updates the list of selectable options.

JavaKubeJS 
selectorWidget.setCandidates(List.of("OptionA", "OptionB", "OptionC"));

selectorWidget.setCandidates(["OptionA", "OptionB", "OptionC"]);
  • Triggers a UI update to reflect the new options.

docs.title: setValue
docs.text:
Sets the currently selected value.

Java / KubeJS 
selectorWidget.setValue("OptionA");
  • If the value is not found in candidates, it remains unchanged.

docs.title: setMaxCount
docs.text:
Defines how many options should be visible before scrolling.

Java / KubeJS 
selectorWidget.setMaxCount(3);
  • If there are more than maxCount options, a scrollbar is added.

docs.title: setFontColor
docs.text:
Changes the color of option text.

Java / KubeJS 
selectorWidget.setFontColor(0xFFFFFF); // White text

docs.title: setButtonBackground
docs.text:
Sets the background texture for the button area.

Java / KubeJS 
selectorWidget.setButtonBackground(myCustomTexture);

docs.title: setOnChanged
docs.text:
Registers a callback to handle selection changes.

JavaKubeJS 
selectorWidget.setOnChanged(selected -> {
    System.out.println("New selection: " + selected);
});

selectorWidget.setOnChanged(selected => {
    console.log("New selection: " + selected);
});
  • This is useful for updating UI state or triggering game logic.

docs.title: setCandidatesSupplier
docs.text:
Automatically updates the option list from a dynamic source.

JavaKubeJS 
selectorWidget.setCandidatesSupplier(() -> fetchDynamicOptions());

selectorWidget.setCandidatesSupplier(() => fetchDynamicOptions());
  • The widget polls this function to refresh the list.
  • Useful when candidates change based on external conditions.

docs.title: setShow
docs.text:
Manually toggles the dropdown visibility.

Java / KubeJS 
selectorWidget.setShow(true); // Opens dropdown

docs.title: SliderWidget
docs.text:
Added v1.0.48

Widget from SliderWidget test.

The SliderWidget is a simple gui slider, like in the Minecraft option menus. It features Horizontal and Vertical modes, and can have any number of steps.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Orientation mode – Horizontal or Vertical modes.
  • Event handling – Fires callbacks when the slider moves.
  • Step Size - How many steps the slider has. 0 = the length of the slider, adding more than the length allows sub gui-pixel slider.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Field Type Description sliderValue Float The position value of the slider, 0 - 1. valueStep Integer Number of steps the slider has. 0 = the length of the slider, adding more than the length allows sub gui-pixel slider. leftUpKey Char/Integer Integer representation of the key used to move the slider in the left or up direction, depending on mode. rightDownKey Char/Integer Same as above but for right and down handleSize Integer Size of handle in pixels. minAmount Float Min amount of slider, used for code only. minAmount Float Same as above but max.

docs.title: setSliderCallback
docs.text:
Setting the change callback. Example sets text overlay to display value in percentage.

JavaKubeJS 
sliderWidget.setSliderCallback(value -> {
    if(sliderWidget.getOverlay() instanceof TextTexture) {
        ((TextTexture) sliderWidget.getOverlay()).updateText(((int) (value * 100)) + "%%");
    }
});

sliderWidget.setSliderCallback(value => {
    if(sliderWidget.getOverlay() typeof TextTexture) {
        ((TextTexture) sliderWidget.getOverlay()).updateText(((int) (value * 100)) + "%%");
    }
});

docs.title: getAmount
docs.text:
Returns float of the lerp from minAmount to maxAmount. Example, minAmount = 5, maxAmount = 10, sliderValue = 0.5f, then .getAmount() returns 0.75f

JavaKubeJS 
value = sliderWidget.getAmount();

value = sliderWidget.getAmount();

docs.title: setAmount
docs.text:
Sets the sliderValue to the inverseLerp using minAmount and maxAmount Example, minAmount = 5, maxAmount = 10, sliderValue = 0.5f, then .getAmount() returns 0.75f

JavaKubeJS 
sliderWidget.setAmount(0.5f);

sliderWidget.setAmount(0.5f);

docs.title: SlotWidget
docs.text:
The SlotWidget represents an interactive item slot within a container GUI. This widget supports configurable properties such as whether items can be taken or placed, customizable hover overlays and tooltips, and integration with JEI/REI/EMI systems for displaying ingredient details. It can be configured to represent a slot in either a standard inventory container or via an item transfer handler.

Note

You cannot modify slot widget size.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description canTakeItems Indicates if items can be extracted from the slot (modifiable via setter) canPutItems Indicates if items can be inserted into the slot (modifiable via setter) drawHoverOverlay Determines whether a hover overlay is drawn when the mouse is over the slot drawHoverTips Determines whether tooltips are shown when the mouse hovers over the slot lastItem Item stored in the last tick.

docs.title: setContainerSlot
docs.text:
Associates the widget with an inventory container by specifying the slot index. e.g. player inventory.

Java / KubeJS 
var player = ...;
slotWidget.setContainerSlot(player.getInventory(), 2); // bind the player inventory of 2 index.

docs.title: setHandlerSlot
docs.text: Configures the widget to use an item transfer handler with the specified slot index.

docs.title: Java
docs.text:
For Java User !!!

If you want to use the handler of ItemStackHandler(from forge) or Storage<ItemVariant>(from fabric), you need one more line. Because Forge and Fabric has different APIs, you need to convert the handler into the LDLib one, which can be found in ItemTransferHelperImpl.

ForgeFabric 
var itemHandler = ...;
var itemTransfer = ItemTransferHelperImpl.toItemTransfer(itemHandler);

var storage = ...;
var itemTransfer = ItemTransferHelperImpl.toItemTransfer(storage);

docs.title: KubeJS
docs.text:
KubeJS user do not need to do such mess. We have already convert them into a item transfer internal already.

KubeJS 
slotWidget.setHandlerSlot(itemTransfer, 0);

docs.title: setItem
docs.text:
Set the internal itemstack with/without notify.

Java / KubeJS 
slotWidget.setItem(itemstack); // it will also trigger the listen you set
slotWidget.setItem(itemstack, false); // it wont trigger the listen

docs.title: getItem
docs.text:
Get the internal itemstack stored.

Java / KubeJS 
var itemstack = slotWidget.getItem();

docs.title: setChangeListener
docs.text:
Configures additional slot location information, such as whether it belongs to the player container or hotbar. It will affect the shift move behaviour.

JavaKubeJS 
slotWidget.setChangeListener(() -> {
    var last = slotWidget.getLastItem();
    var current = slotWidget.getItem();
});

slotWidget.setChangeListener(() => {
    let last = slotWidget.getLastItem();
    let current = slotWidget.getItem();
});

docs.title: canPutStack
docs.text:
Whether can put stack into the slot.

Java / KubeJS 
slotWidget.canPutStack(true);

docs.title: canTakeStack
docs.text:
Whether can take stack from the slot.

Java / KubeJS 
slotWidget.canTakeStack(true);

docs.title: setLocationInfo
docs.text:
Configures additional slot location information, such as whether it belongs to the player container or hotbar. It will affect the shift move behaviour.

Java / KubeJS 
slotWidget.setLocationInfo(true, false); // (isPlayerContainer isPlayerHotBar)

docs.title: SwitchWidget
docs.text: The SwitchWidget is a toggle button that switches between an ON and OFF state. It supports custom textures, event callbacks, and dynamic state updates.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Toggle button behavior – Click to switch between ON and OFF.
  • Event handling – Fires callbacks when the switch state changes.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Field Type Description isPressed boolean (default: false) Current switch state.

docs.title: setPressed
docs.text:
Sets the ON/OFF state of the switch.

Java / KubeJS 
switchWidget.setPressed(true); // Turns ON
  • Triggers UI updates and event callbacks.

docs.title: setOnPressCallback
docs.text:
Registers a callback when the switch is clicked.

JavaKubeJS 
switchWidget.setOnPressCallback((clickData, state) -> {
    System.out.println("Switch is now: " + state);
});

switchWidget.setOnPressCallback((clickData, state) => {
    console.log("Switch is now: " + state);
});

docs.title: setSupplier
docs.text:
Automatically syncs with an external state.

JavaKubeJS 
switchWidget.setSupplier(() -> getCurrentState()); // bool

switchWidget.setSupplier(() => getCurrentState()); // bool
  • Updates dynamically when getCurrentState() changes.

docs.title: TankWidget
docs.text: The TankWidget represents a fluid slot widget within a container GUI. It displays a fluid in a designated tank and supports fluid interactions such as filling and emptying. In addition, it integrates with external systems (JEI/REI/EMI) to show fluid ingredient details and provides configurable options for hover overlays, tooltips, and click behavior.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description fluidTank The fluid storage or transfer handler associated with this widget tank The index of the fluid tank being represented showAmount Determines whether the fluid amount is displayed allowClickFilled Controls whether clicking the slot when filled triggers container-filling behavior allowClickDrained Controls whether clicking the slot when empty triggers container-emptying behavior drawHoverOverlay Indicates if a hover overlay should be drawn when the mouse is over the widget drawHoverTips Indicates if hover tooltips should be displayed fillDirection The direction in which the fluid fill is rendered (e.g. DOWN_TO_UP) lastFluidInTank Last fluid stored in the tank lastTankCapacity Last tank capacity

docs.title: setFluidTank (IFluidStorage)
docs.text:
Associates the widget with a fluid storage and sets the tank index to 0.

Java / KubeJS 
tankWidget.setFluidTank(fluidStorage);

docs.title: setFluidTank (IFluidTransfer, int)
docs.text:
Associates the widget with a fluid transfer handler and specifies the tank index.

Java / KubeJS 
tankWidget.setFluidTank(fluidTransfer, 1);

docs.title: setFluid
docs.text:
Set the internal fluidstack with/without notify.

Java / KubeJS 
tankWidget.setFluid(fluidstack); // it will also trigger the listen you set
tankWidget.setFluid(fluidstack, false); // it wont trigger the listen

docs.title: getFluid
docs.text:
Get the internal fluidstack stored.

Java / KubeJS 
var fluidstack = tankWidget.getFluid();

docs.title: setChangeListener
docs.text:
Configures additional slot location information, such as whether it belongs to the player container or hotbar. It will affect the shift move behaviour.

JavaKubeJS 
tankWidget.setChangeListener(() -> {
    var last = tankWidget.getLastFluidInTank();
    var current = tankWidget.getFluid();
});

slotWidget.setChangeListener(() => {
    let last = tankWidget.getLastFluidInTank();
    let current = tankWidget.getFluid();
});

docs.title: TextFieldWidget
docs.text:
Your browser does not support video.

The TextFieldWidget provides an editable text field for GUI interfaces. It supports dynamic text updates via a supplier and responder, validation through custom validators, and configurable properties such as maximum string length, border style, and text color.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description currentString The current text displayed by the text field maxStringLength Maximum allowed length for the text isBordered Determines whether the text field has a border textColor The color of the text (modifiable via setter) supplier A supplier for dynamic text updates textResponder A responder that handles text changes wheelDur Duration (or step value) used for mouse wheel adjustments

docs.title: setTextSupplier
docs.text:
Sets the supplier used to update the text dynamically.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setTextSupplier(() -> "Dynamic Text");

docs.title: setTextResponder
docs.text:
Sets the responder to be called when the text changes.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setTextResponder(newText -> {
    // Handle text change
});

docs.title: setBordered
docs.text:
Configures whether the text field should display a border.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setBordered(true);

docs.title: setTextColor
docs.text:
Sets the text color for the text field.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setTextColor(0xffffff);

docs.title: setMaxStringLength
docs.text:
Sets the maximum number of characters allowed in the text field.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setMaxStringLength(100);

docs.title: setValidator
docs.text:
Assigns a custom validator function to control and sanitize text input.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setValidator(text -> text.trim());

docs.title: setCompoundTagOnly
docs.text:
Restricts input to valid compound tags. Displays a tooltip indicating the restriction.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setCompoundTagOnly();

docs.title: setResourceLocationOnly
docs.text:
Restricts input to valid resource locations. Displays a tooltip indicating the restriction.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setResourceLocationOnly();

docs.title: setNumbersOnly
docs.text:
Restricts input to numeric values. Overloads are available for different numeric types.

JavaKubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setNumbersOnly(0, 100); // int
textFieldWidget.setNumbersOnly(0.0f, 1.0f); // float

textFieldWidget.setNumbersOnlyInt(0, 100); // int
textFieldWidget.setNumbersOnlyFloat(0, 100); // float

docs.title: setWheelDur
docs.text:
Sets the wheel duration (step value) for adjusting numbers via mouse wheel or dragging.

Java / KubeJS 
textFieldWidget.setWheelDur(1);

docs.title: TextTextureWidget
docs.text: TextTextureWidget is an advanced text widget compared with LabelWidget. It wrapper an internal TextTexture, therefore, you can set the all text rendering properties by it.

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text: Field Description lastComponent The last component text displayed read only textTexture Internal TextTexture read only

docs.title: textureStyle
docs.text:
Modifies the style of the internal text texture. see TextTexture for more details.

JavaKubeJS 
textTextureWidget.textureStyle(texture -> {

The ProgressWidget is a UI component that visually represents progress using a progress bar. It can be used in various contexts, such as tracking crafting progress, energy levels, or other dynamically changing values.
